project: Atlas - Assassin's Creed Shadows Interactive Map

deploymentState.summary: The release manifest, entry page, bootstrap and service-worker version are synchronized at 0.9.4.4. However, repository evidence does not yet prove that every declared cumulative runtime owner is loaded and executed by the public production path, and post-deployment public/device verification remains required.

conversationHistoryCoverage.limitation: No additional retrievable project conversation archive was available during this run; previously recorded requests and commitments remain authoritative.

evidence: index.html visible version and asset query strings are 0.9.4.4
atlas-bootstrap.js owns service-worker registration and update
automatic recovery stopped safely for one unknown OpenCV media-open failure
original media retention and authorisation scope were not broadened

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: The public release must display and load one consistent intended Atlas version.
evidence: release manifest, entry page and bootstrap now report 0.9.4.4
public response and active controller still require post-deployment verification

knownRegressions.acceptedBehaviourAffected: Previously accepted liquid navigation, controls and iPad-specific behaviour must remain present after release changes.
evidence: release-manifest.json declares atlas-liquid-nav-0934.js, atlas-controls-0938.js and atlas-ipad-nav-0940.js as release assets/runtime owners
index.html does not explicitly reference those three files
no inspected browser-resource evidence proves that the production path loads and executes each owner exactly once

Deployment version-source mismatch confirmed; release reclassified as regressed and unverified.
